name: Dill Salmon Dip
directions: In medium bowl, combine soup, mayonnaise, horse-radish, dill and pepper.
Mix well.
Add salmon; mix lightly.
Cover, chill at lease 15 minutes.
Serve with vegetables or chips.

name: Spinach Dip With Green Onion
directions: Thaw and squeeze spinach until dry; chop.
In mixer add all ingredients together and mix well.
Cover and refrigerate 2 hours before serving.
You may want to add only 1 cup of sour cream then see if you want to add the rest.

name: Creamy Bacon-Ranch Pasta Salad
directions: Mix mayo and ranch seasoning until well blended.
Set aside.
In large bowl combine pasta, tomatos, cucumbers, and mayo mixture.
Add salt, pepper, and bacon bits.
Let pasta salad set 4-6 hours or overnight if possible.
Add more mayo if needed before serving (the creamier the better!).
Serve chilled.
My husband is a cheese lover and likes me to add 2 cups of shredded sharp cheddar.
For a change of color, I use garden blend spiral pasta.

name: Tomato Shrimp Mousse
directions: Heat soup just to boiling.
Soften gelatine in cold water in a large bowl.
Pour hot soup over gelatine.
Stir until dissolved.
Beat in cream cheese and next 4 ingredients.
Stir in shrimp, celery and onions.
Pour mixture into a 1 1/2 quart mold.
Chill until firm.
Can be refrigerated over night or 24 hours.
Unmold on platter.
This can be used as a spread for crackers for hors d' oeuvres or molded in a square Pyrex casserole and cut out in separate servings as an entree for luncheon or dinner.
Serve on lettuce leaf with a green vegetable and fresh fruit.

name: Tortilla Chip Dip
directions: Combine beans and chili powder; mix well.  This is the first layer.
Combine avocados, salad dressing, bacon, onion and seasonings; mix well.  This is your 2nd layer.
Your next layer (3rd) is chopped olives.
Your next layer (4th) is chopped tomatoes.
Your next layer (5th) is chopped chilies.
Your last (top) layer is your cheese.
Put all in a shallow clear glass dish (for a beautiful presentation), layered as indicated.
Serve with tortilla chips.

name: Bow Tie Pasta Salad
directions: Cook pasta according to package directions; drain and rinse with cold water.
Toss tomatoes, cucumber,black olives, olive oil, garlic, basil and salt and pepper.
In large bowl, gently toss the pasta with the tomato mixture and Mozzarella cheese.
Chill for 2-3 hours so flavors can blend.

name: Cucumber Hors D'oeuvres
directions: Mix salad dressing and cream cheese.
Spread onto bread.
Peel and slice cucumber (@1/8inch thick).
If you want, leave strips of green on the cucumber (looks nice) Place 1 slice of cucumber onto bread.
Sprinkle with Dill.

name: BLT Salad
directions: Combine mayo yogurt chili lemon juice sugar and salt in small bowl.
In a large bowl combine remaining ingredients.
Mix all.
Cover and chill.

name: Balsamic Pasta Salad
directions: In a covered glass jar, combine vinegar, water, oil, salt, pepper and garlic.
Shake vigorously.
I usually place the ingredients right into my food processor to blend.
Set aside.
Heat water in a large saucepan, add broccoli, cauliflower and carrots.
Cook for 30 seconds; drain.
Pour cold water over vegetables to stop the cooking process; drain.
In a salad bowl, combine vegetables, bell pepper, pasta, basil and cheese.
Now blend in vinegar mixture; toss gently.
Cover and chill until serving.

name: Spicy Coleslaw
directions: Toss cabbage, onion, carrot and parsley until well mixed.
In a small bowl mix the oil, vinegar, mayonnaise, sugar and seasonings.
Stir to mix well.
Pour the dressing over the cabbage mixture and toss gently to mix.
Chill for at least 2 hours before serving.

name: Dill Shrimp Salad
directions: In a Bowl, combine the shrimp, water chestnuts, onion, dill, and Italian dressing; blend well.
Cover and refrigerate for 1½ to 2 hours, or until well chilled.
For each serving, place one fourth of the Lettuce on each plate.
Top it with the marinated Shrimp mixture, then garnish with the wedges of tomato.
May also be garnished with sprig of Fresh Parsley, or Fresh Basil leaves.

name: Easy Refrigerator pickles
directions: Mix sugar, vinegar,salt,spices, DO NOT HEAT.
Sterilize 3 large jars, (like peanut butter).
Place one sliced onion in each jar, fill with sliced cucumbers.
Pour syrup to top ofjar and put on lids.
Place in refrigerator.
Ready to eat in 5 days.
Keeps 1 year in refrigerator.

name: Buttermilk Fruit Salad
directions: Combine vanilla pudding and buttermilk; mix well.
Add all of the fruits and Cool Whip.
Most all fruits taste good in this, so add whatever fits your personal preferences.
Also good with mini-marshmallows.
Mix all ingredients together and chill for at least an hour.
I prefer to make the night before.
Enjoy!
If you are making for a large crowd, recipe doubles very easily!

name: Deli-Style Pasta Salad
directions: Cook pasta according to directions; rinse in cold water, drain.
Place the cooked pasta in a large bowl.
Toss with 1-2 tbsp olive oil (to prevent pasta from sticking and drying out, this is optional).
Add the next 9 ingredients, with the Parmesan cheese; toss to combine.
In a jar with a tight fitting lid, combine oil, vinegar, garlic powder, mustard, basil, oregano, salt and pepper; shake well to combine.
Add in sugar if you find that the dressing is too strong.
Pour over the salad; toss to coat well.
Cover, and chill for 8 or more hours, (overnight is even better).
Toss again before serving.

name: Lorna Doone Cookie Dessert
directions: Mix crushed cookies with melted margarine.
Put in a 9 x 13 inch pan.
Bake at 325 degrees for 10 minutes.
Cool.
In a mixer, beat pudding with milk.
Add ice cream and blend.
Spread over cookie mixture.
Cover with Cool Whip.
Sprinkle crushed bars over top and refrigerate.

name: Peachy Coleslaw
directions: In a large bowl, combine all ingredients; mix well to combine.
Refrigerate for 1 or more hours to blend flavors.
Note: if desired 1 (8 oz) pkg pre-shredded coleslaw can replace the 6 cups of shredded cabbage in the recipe.

name: Louisiana Mustard
directions: In a small mixing bowl, add the mustard and flour; stir to mix.
Gradually stir in the water; let stand for 15 minutes.
Whisk in the remaining ingredients.
Transfer mixture to an air-tight container; may store in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

name: Party Rye Appetizers
directions: In a mixing bowl, combine the cream cheese and dressing mix.
Spread on slices of party rye bread, then top with cucumber slices.
Refrigerate until ready to serve.

name: Jambalaya Salad
directions: Combine first eight ingredients.
Add remaining ingredients except bacon.
Chill and add bacon just before serving.

name: Eggs Stuffed With Crabmeat
directions: Cut eggs in half lengthwise; carefully remove yolks and mash; set egg whites aside.
Combine egg yolks, crabmeat and the next 5 ingredients; stirring well to combine.
Spoon mixture into egg whites.
Sprinkle with paprika.
Cover and chill until ready to serve.
